page: Vultr vbm-24c-384gb-amd is a bare metal server configured with an AMD EPYC 9254 processor built on the x86_64 architecture. The system features 48 dedicated physical cores operating at a base frequency of 2.9 GHz, with one thread per core. Memory resources consist of 384.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 RAM running at 6 GHz, yielding a resource density of 8.0 GB of memory per physical core. Local storage is provided by a 960 GB solid-state drive (SSD). The server does not include any graphics processing units (GPUs) but comes with one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Benchmark evaluations place this server's performance in high tiers across multiple categories. In single-core CPU testing via stress-ng, the processor achieves a top-tier rating within the top 10% of tested systems, while multi-core performance falls within the top 25%. Memory subsystem performance is notable, with PassMark memory scores and large-block uncached write bandwidth both ranking in the top 10% tier. Cryptographic and compression capabilities are also strong, with single-threaded bzip3 decompression reaching the top 10% tier and PassMark encryption tests placing in the top 25%. Database operations, measured via Redis SET operations and PassMark database tests, consistently perform in the top 25%.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the bare metal architecture eliminates virtualization overhead, maximizing the return on the 48 dedicated physical cores and DDR5 memory. However, the lack of hyperthreading (one thread per core) and the absence of a GPU represent clear tradeoffs for workloads requiring massive parallel thread scaling or hardware-accelerated machine learning. The 960 GB SSD provides local storage but may require external network storage for massive datasets. This hardware profile is suited for memory-intensive applications, database hosting, high-throughput web serving, and heavy cryptographic or compression workloads.
description: Vultr vbm-24c-384gb-amd is a bare metal server featuring an AMD EPYC 9254 processor with 48 dedicated physical cores running at 2.9 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. It is equipped with 384.0 GB of DDR5 RAM operating at 6 GHz and a 960 GB local SSD, with no GPU acceleration. Benchmark data indicates top-tier performance, with single-core CPU stress-ng, large-block uncached memory write bandwidth, and PassMark memory scores all ranking in the top 10%. Multi-core CPU performance, database operations, and static web serving throughput consistently place in the top 25%. The absence of hyperthreading and GPU hardware are key tradeoffs. This configuration is suited for memory-intensive databases, cryptographic processing, and high-throughput web serving.
og_description: Vultr vbm-24c-384gb-amd is a bare metal server with a 48-core AMD EPYC 9254 CPU, 384 GB DDR5 RAM, and a 960 GB SSD, delivering top-tier memory bandwidth and strong database performance.
meta_description: Vultr vbm-24c-384gb-amd is a bare metal server featuring 48 AMD EPYC cores, 384 GB DDR5 RAM, and a 960 GB SSD for memory-intensive workloads.
tagline: A dedicated bare metal server combining forty-eight physical AMD EPYC cores with high-speed DDR5 memory for demanding database workloads.
bullet_points: AMD EPYC 9254 processor with 48 dedicated physical cores at 2.9 GHz
384.0 GB of high-speed 6 GHz DDR5 RAM (8.0 GB per core)
960 GB local SSD storage and one public IPv4 address
Top-tier memory write bandwidth and PassMark memory scores
Strong multi-core CPU and database operation benchmarks
Suited for memory-intensive databases and cryptographic processing
